folio.label: 146r
folio.l: Lo rathere he forbedetú , swicú sweryngö
Than homicide , or many a cursed thyngö
I seye , that as by ordre , thus it standitú
This knowen they , that hise hestes vndirstonditú
How that the secunde heest of god , is that
And forthere ouere / I wol the telle al plat
That vengeau~ce , shal nat parten , fro his hous
That of hise othes , is to outrageous
By goddes päcious herte , and by his nayles
And by the blod of Crist , that is in hayles
Seuene is my chaunce , and thyn synk and treye
By goddes armes , if thow falsely pleye
This dagger shal , thurgú out thyn herte goo
This fruyt cometú , of the becched bones two
Forsweryngö , Ire , falsnesse , homycyde
Now for the loue of Crist , that for vs dyede
Lete ©oure othes , bothe grete and smale
But Sires , now wol I telle fortú my tale
¢ Theise riotoures thre , of whicú I telle
Longe erst or pryme rongö , of any belle
Weren set hem in a Tauäne , to drynke
And as they sat , they herde a belle clynke
Byforn~ a cors , was caried to his graue
That on of hem , gan callen to his knaue
Go bet q¶ he , and axe redily
What cors is this , that passetú heere forby
And loke that thow , reporte his name wel
Sire quod this boy , it nedetú neuäe a del
¢ It was me told , or ©e come heere , two houres
He was parde , an olde felawe of ©oures
And sodeynly , he was I slayn to nygút
For dronke as he sat , on his benche vp rigút
Ther come a pryue thef , men clepen detú
That in this contree , al the peeple sleetú
And witú his spere , he smot his herte a twoo
And went his wey , witú oute wordes moo
He hatú a thousand slayn , this pestilence
And maister or ©e come , in his presence
Me thynketú that it were , necessarie
For to be war , of swicú an aduäsarie
Betú redy for to mete hi~ , euäemoore
Thus taugút me my dame , I sey nomore
¢ By Seynt Marie , seide this Tauernere
The child seitú sotú , for he hatú slayn this ©ere
